title: What The $2.2 Billion BYD Deal Means For Jabil Shareholders
text: JBL is selling its Mobility China business to BYD for $2.4 billion. That equates to an estimated 16% of Jabil's entire market cap and an estimated ~15% of Jabil's entire enterprise value. Even if the company decides to allocate only 50% of the proceeds to buybacks, it will more than double the existing authorization to an estimated $1.9 billion.

title: Jabil (JBL) Stock Jumps 9% on BYD Deal
text: Shares of design engineering services Jabil (NYSE: JBL ) — which primarily specializes in electronics-related equipment — jumped sharply on Monday morning. Earlier, Chinese automaker BYD (OTCMKTS: BYDDY ) announced that it would buy Jabil's China-based mobile electronics manufacturing business in China for 15.8 billion yuan ($2.2 billion).

title: Jabil Cuts Exposure To Apple, China With Division Sale
text: Contract manufacturer Jabil will lower its exposure to major customer Apple and operations in China with the planned sale of its mobility unit.

title: Jabil Shares Jump After Agreeing to Sell Its Mobility Business to BYD for $2.2 Billion
text: Shares of U.S.-based manufacturing company Jabil (JBL) jumped over 7% in early trading on Monday after it agreed to sell its mobility business to the electronics arm of EV maker and Apple vendor BYD for $2.2 billion.

title: BYD Electronic plans to buy Jabil manufacturing subsidiary for nearly $2.2 billion
text: BYD Electronic is planning to buy a company that could own a manufacturing business in the Chinese cities of Chengdu and Wuxi for nearly $2.2 billion to expand its smartphone components.

title: EV maker BYD buys Jabil's China manufacturing business for $2.2 billion
text: Electric vehicle maker BYD Co Ltd said on Monday its unit struck a deal with U.S.-based manufacturing firm Jabil Inc's Singaporean unit to buy its product manufacturing business in China for 15.8 billion yuan ($2.17 billion).
